# Fuse Engram embedding weights tile by tile

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_deepseek_manual.py; do not edit. -->

This Engram translation keeps lookup, gating, or gradient work in typed tiles.

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Upstream module | [`tile_kernels/engram/engram_fused_weight_kernel.py`](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/TileKernels/blob/36d9e45d38e204ebb87e6f6e833821eee0482fe5/tile_kernels/engram/engram_fused_weight_kernel.py) |
| Pinned revision | `36d9e45d38e204ebb87e6f6e833821eee0482fe5` |
| C++ function | `fused_weight` |
| Verification | `source-validated` |
| Minimum thread fragment | 128 bytes |

## Core C++ kernel

```cpp title="fused_weight_pto.hpp" linenums="1"
template <int HcMult, int Hidden, int TileW = 64>
void fused_weight(__bf16 *weight_hidden, __bf16 *weight_embed, float *weight_fused) {
    static_assert(HcMult > 0 && Hidden > 0, "dim must be positive");
    static_assert(TileW * 2 % 32 == 0, "TileW(bf16) must be 32B aligned");
    constexpr int kTiles = Hidden / TileW;
    using namespace pto;
    using gm_bf = global_tensor<__bf16, RowMajor<HcMult, Hidden>>;
    using gm_f  = global_tensor<float,   RowMajor<HcMult, Hidden>>;
    using tile_bf = LocalTile< __bf16, 1, TileW, BLayout::RowMajor>;
    using tile_f  = LocalTile< float,  1, TileW, BLayout::RowMajor>;
    using it_bf = global_iterator<gm_bf, tile_bf>;
    using it_f  = global_iterator<gm_f,  tile_f>;
    it_bf h_iter(weight_hidden);
    it_bf e_iter(weight_embed);
    it_f  o_iter(weight_fused);
    for (int h = 0; h < HcMult; ++h) {
        for (int t = 0; t < kTiles; ++t) {
            auto gh = h_iter(h, t); auto ge = e_iter(h, t); auto go = o_iter(h, t);
            tile_bf a, b; tile_f af, bf, of;
            TLOAD(a, gh);
            TLOAD(b, ge);
            TCVT(af, a);
            TCVT(bf, b);
            TMUL(of, af, bf);
            TSTORE(go, of);
        }
    }
}
```

## Algorithm walkthrough

Load the thread-owned token and weight fragments, apply the documented gate, hash, or reduction stages, then store the valid result region.

## Source-to-intrinsic mapping

[`TCVT`](../../../intrinsics/tcvt.md), [`TLOAD`](../../../intrinsics/tload.md), [`TMUL`](../../../intrinsics/tmul.md), [`TSTORE`](../../../intrinsics/tstore.md)

Scalar C++ controls dimensions, loop bounds, and tile coordinates. Tensor
values remain in global tensor views and typed tiles throughout the data path.

## Block and thread partition

The block partitions tokens across threads. Each active thread owns contiguous, non-overlapping token rows with at least 128 bytes of valid tile data.

## Memory and tile flow

Global tensor views feed thread-local tiles. Arithmetic and reductions create new tile versions before the final result is stored.

## Build and verification

Run the migration gate from the repository root:

```console
python3 scripts/verify_deepseek_migration.py
```

The verification label records the strongest completed gate. A
`source-validated` page is a valid API example even when compiler lowering
for one of its public intrinsics is not yet available.

    void fused_weight(__bf16 *weight_hidden, __bf16 *weight_embed, float *weight_fused) {
        static_assert(HcMult > 0 && Hidden > 0, "dim must be positive");
        static_assert(TileW * 2 % 32 == 0, "TileW(bf16) must be 32B aligned");

        using tile_f  = LocalTile< float,  1, TileW, BLayout::RowMajor>;

        it_bf e_iter(weight_embed);

                tile_bf a, b; tile_f af, bf, of;
                TLOAD(a, gh);
                TLOAD(b, ge);
                TCVT(af, a);
                TCVT(bf, b);
                TMUL(of, af, bf);
